MEMORANDM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 100044436) FILED BY PLAINTIFF RICO, STEPHANIE

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AUG-22-2018 HEARING HELD ON DEFENDANT LUIS VERA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION IS GRANTED. VIEWING THE UNDISPUTED FACTS IN THE LIGHT MOST FAVORABLE TO PLAINTIFF STEPHANIE RICO, THOSE FACTS ARE INSUFFICIENT TO SHOW THAT MR. VERA HAD A DUTY TO PROTECT MS. VERA FROM THE ASSAULT AND BATTERY SHE SUFFERED FROM A CO-PASSENGER IN THE VEHICLE DRIVEN BY MR. VERA. THE UNDISPUTED FACTS DO NOT SUPPORT THE EXISTENCE OF A SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN MR. VERA AND MS. RICO FOR PURPOSES OF IMPOSING A DUTY ON MR. VERA TO PROTECT MS. VERA FROM THE UNANTICIPATED VIOLENT CONDUCT OF MS. ABRAHAM. (CALIFORNIA BODY & TRAILER MFRS. V. ALBRECHT (1971) 16 CAL.APP.3D SUPP. 1, 3-4 ("THE DRIVER OF AN AUTOMOBILE MUST MANAGE HIS VEHICLE SO AS NOT TO CREATE AN UNREASONABLE RISK OF HARM TO OTHERS, BUT IN THE ABSENCE OF FACTS PUTTING THE DRIVER [ON NOTICE OF LIKELY MISCONDUCT OF ANOTHER PASSENGER]. . .THE DRIVER IS UNDER NO DUTY TO PERSONS WHO MAY BE INJURED BY THE NEGLIGENCE OF THE GUEST. ORDINARILY, IN THE ABSENCE OF A SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PARTIES, THERE IS NO DUTY TO CONTROL THE CONDUCT OF A THIRD PERSON SO AS TO PREVENT HIM FROM CAUSING HARM TO ANOTHER.") (CITATIONS OMITTED)). A PROPOSED ORDER WAS SUBMITTED TO THE COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-13-2022 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; Clerk: G.Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-20-2022 Continued To Dec-13-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-10-2018 Set For Order To Show Cause On Oct-25-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

ARBITRATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Continued To Dec-18-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-22-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James B. Sutherland, Esq. of the Law Office of James Bachan, 510.286.8900, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Mark Carbone, Esq. of Carbone Smoke Smith Bent & Leonard, 510.267.7270, appearing for the Defendant Vera.

Ruling: ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Luis Vera's motion for summary judgment is granted. Viewing the undisputed facts in the light most favorable to plaintiff Stephanie Rico, those facts are insufficient to show that Mr. Vera had a duty to protect Ms. Vera from the assault and battery she suffered from a co-passenger in the vehicle driven by Mr. Vera. The undisputed facts do not support the existence of a special relationship between Mr. Vera and Ms. Rico for purposes of imposing a duty on Mr. Vera to protect Ms. Vera from the unanticipated violent conduct of Ms. Abraham. (California Body & Trailer Mfrs. v. Albrecht (1971) 16 Cal.App.3d Supp. 1, 3-4 ("The driver of an automobile must manage his vehicle so as not to create an unreasonable risk of harm to others, but in the absence of facts putting the driver [on notice of likely misconduct of another passenger]. . .the driver is under no duty to persons who may be injured by the negligence of the guest. Ordinarily, in the absence of a special relationship between the parties, there is no duty to control the conduct of a third person so as to prevent him from causing harm to another.") (citations omitted)). A proposed order was submitted to the Court.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/HEK)
